county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jasper County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Jasper). County seat is Jasper. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Jasper. Original Texas county (East Texas). Jasper County Historical Commission. Deep East Texas timber heritage. Angelina National Forest records. Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC. Significant African American community.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Jasper County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Jasper holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1836. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Jasper County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Jasper County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Jasper County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Jasper County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jeff Davis County formed 1887 from Presidio. County seat is Fort Davis. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Fort Davis. Trans-Pecos county. Fort Davis National Historic Site — Buffalo Soldiers (9th and 10th Cavalry) records at NARA. Jeff Davis County Historical Society. Ranching heritage. Check Presidio for pre-1887 records. Davis Mountains area. Chihuahuan Desert Research Institute.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Jeff Davis County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1887; County Clerk in Fort Davis holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Jeff Davis County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1887 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Jeff Davis County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1887. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Jeff Davis County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1887. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Jeff Davis County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1887 to 1950 for Jeff Davis County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Jeff Davis County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Jeff Davis County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Jeff Davis County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Jeff Davis County from 1887. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jefferson County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Jefferson). County seat is Beaumont. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Beaumont. Original Texas county (Gulf Coast). Texas Energy Museum (Spindletop oil discovery, 1901). Tyrrell Historical Library in Beaumont is a major repository. McFaddin-Ward House archives. Significant African American community. Cajun heritage. Port Arthur records. Lamar University archives. Oil industry transformed Jefferson County (1901+).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Jefferson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Beaumont holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Jefferson County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Jefferson County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Jefferson County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Beaumont
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1901. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Jefferson County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Jefferson County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jim Hogg County formed 1913 from Brooks, Duval. County seat is Hebbronville. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hebbronville. South Texas ranching county created 1913. Jim Hogg County Historical Commission. Strong Mexican-American heritage. Check Brooks and Duval for pre-1913 records. Oil and gas exploration.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Jim Hogg County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1913; County Clerk in Hebbronville holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jim Wells County formed 1911 from Nueces. County seat is Alice. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Alice. South Texas county. Jim Wells County Heritage Museum. King Ranch territory. Check Nueces for pre-1911 records. Strong Mexican-American heritage. Alice was a ranching center.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Johnson County formed 1854 from McLennan, Navarro. County seat is Cleburne. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Cleburne. North Texas county. Layland Museum in Cleburne. Johnson County Historical Commission. Check McLennan and Navarro for pre-1854 records. Railroad heritage (Santa Fe). Chisholm Trail route.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Johnson County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1854; County Clerk in Cleburne holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jones County formed 1858 from Bexar, Bosque. County seat is Anson. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Anson. Rolling Plains county organized 1881. Jones County Historical Commission. Fort Phantom Hill (frontier post) ruins. Cowboys' Christmas Ball heritage. Check Bexar and Bosque for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Jones County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Anson holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Karnes County formed 1854 from Bexar, DeWitt, Goliad, San Patricio. County seat is Karnes City. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Karnes City. South-central Texas county. Karnes County Historical Society. Helena (original county seat, now ghost town). Polish settlement (Panna Maria, 1854 — first Polish settlement in America). Check Bexar, DeWitt, Goliad, San Patricio for pre-1854 records. Immaculate Conception Church in Panna Maria has records from 1855.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Karnes County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1854; County Clerk in Karnes City holds deed books

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Kaufman. North Texas county. Kaufman County Historical Commission. Check Henderson for pre-1848 records. Agricultural heritage. Significant African American community.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kendall County formed 1862 from Blanco, Kerr. County seat is Boerne. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Boerne. Hill Country county with German heritage (part of the New Braunfels/Comfort German settlement corridor). Kendall County Historical Commission. Comfort Heritage Foundation (German Freethinkers/Unionists — Treue der Union monument, Civil War loyalty conflict). Check Blanco and Kerr for pre-1862 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Kendall County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1862; County Clerk in Boerne holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1862 to 1950 for Kendall County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Kendall County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Kendall County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Kendall County from 1862. GLO archives include searchable online databases.
